Barack Obama: Barack Hussein Obama II (was born 4 August, 1961) is an American politician who served as the 44th president of America from 2009 until 2,017. Being a member of the Democratic Party, he was the first African American president. He received a Bachelor of Arts degree in political science from Columbia University in 1983, and then he worked as a community organizer in Chicago. In 1988, he joined Harvard Law School, where he was the first Black president of the Harvard Law Review. He used the results of participating as a teacher of politics and a teacher of community.
